Theorem 1.5. Riemann-Stieltjes integrability criterion.

A bounded real-valued function \(f\) defined on the interval \([a, b]\) is Riemann-Stieltjes integrable iff for any \(\epsilon > 0\text{,}\) there exists a partition \(\cP\) of \([a, b]\) such that
\begin{equation} U(f, \cP, d\alpha) - L(f, \cP, d\alpha) < \epsilon.\tag{1.1} \end{equation}
